task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: Service tools

abstract.p: Let us look at the tools available to a service technician.
Part of the tools can be used as preparation and part of the tools can be done during the service visit.
Preparation should be used to make sure you have the tools, parts, and time needed for the service visit.

li.p: There are a lot of manuals and videos available for download and consultation.
The TSM ready for use, and it offers a lot of solutions for error messages and common problems on the printer.
There are several application guides and white papers available for additional help.
Via ORS or local connection you can contact the print system and get service (worklist) information.
Via ORS or local connection you can get error information from the print system.
For each error in the system there is a link to an error document (in the TSM) that will allow you to fix the error.
Via ORS or local connection you can look into the digital logbook that contains all statistics from the print system.
You will find statistics on all error that occurred in the print system.
If deemed necessary you should fix the errors.
Via remote assistance you can take over the User interface of the customer (after his approval).
You can check all setting and the behavior of the system before you go there.
The SDS provides you with a tool to check/operate most parts of the print system.
Via ORS or local connection you can see if maintenance is due on the print system.
If so it can now be carried out now. All required materials should be available if you prepared your visit.
